name: Quercetin Phytosome
historicalUse: See Quercetin Dihydrate monograph for full historical context. The phytosome form is a modern bioavailability-enhancement technology applied to the same active compound. Quercetin-rich plants — onion, elder, buckwheat — have been used across Greek, Roman, Arabic, and Chinese medicinal traditions for millennia.
modernEvidence: Quercetin phytosome (quercetin bound to phosphatidylcholine) achieves 20-50x greater oral bioavailability compared to standard quercetin aglycone, enabling clinically meaningful plasma concentrations at significantly lower doses. Bioavailability data from comparative pharmacokinetic studies supports its use in conditions requiring measurable serum quercetin levels. Senolytic activity, mast cell stabilization, and Nrf2 activation are preserved with enhanced tissue delivery.
mechanisms: Enhanced bioavailability — phospholipid complex enables 20-50x greater systemic absorption vs standard quercetin
Mast cell stabilization — inhibits IgE-mediated degranulation and histamine release
NLRP3 inflammasome inhibition — reduces IL-1β and IL-18 processing
Senolytic activity — selectively induces apoptosis in senescent cells at achievable plasma concentrations
Zinc ionophore function — facilitates intracellular zinc transport
Nrf2 activation — upregulates glutathione synthesis and antioxidant enzymes
benefits: Supports healthy inflammatory balance with superior tissue bioavailability
Supports seasonal respiratory and allergic comfort
Contributes to cellular senescence support pathways
Supports antioxidant defense at effective tissue concentrations
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Phytosome form is well tolerated. Same safety considerations as quercetin dihydrate apply, with note that phytosome achieves higher plasma levels — interactions are more clinically relevant than with standard quercetin.
contraindications: Estrogen-sensitive conditions — quercetin has mild estrogenic activity
drugInteractions: Cyclosporine — quercetin inhibits CYP3A4; phytosome achieves higher plasma levels increasing interaction significance; monitor cyclosporine trough levels
Warfarin — monitor INR; phytosome form may have greater interaction potential than standard quercetin
physicianNotes: The enhanced bioavailability of phytosome quercetin makes drug interactions more clinically significant than with standard quercetin. Exercise heightened caution in transplant patients on cyclosporine — even modest CYP3A4 inhibition at higher plasma quercetin concentrations may alter cyclosporine trough levels meaningfully. Specify phospholipid source for soy-allergic patients.
patientGuidance: Take with meals. If you take immunosuppressant medications, blood thinners, or antibiotics, inform your physician. This is an enhanced-absorption form of quercetin — a smaller amount achieves more than standard quercetin supplements.
